revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:京の大仏]]
{{Otheruses||The Great Buddha of Kyoto (Kyunoin-ji Great Buddha) that existed in Kyoto from the Heian to the Muromachi periods|{{ill|Ungoji Temple (Kyoto)|ja|雲居寺_(京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ungoji Temple (Kyoto)|qq=}}}} [[File:Hokoji(rushanabutsu).jpg|thumb|250px|The current main deity of Hōkō-ji, the seated statue of {{ill|Vairocana Buddha|ja|毘盧遮那仏|lt=Rushanabutsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vairocana Buddha|1=Birushana Buddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17193861}}. It is said to be a 1/10 scale replica of the Great Buddha from the past{{Sfn|Murayama|2003|p=159}}. After the third Great Buddha was destroyed, this statue was consecrated, making it a replica of the third Great Buddha. However, some traditions claim it represents the second Great Buddha built by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|en|Toyotomi Hideyori|qid=Q313641|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}} (as the appearances of the second and third Great Buddhas differed, see below)<ref name="Name-less-pYxu-1">Obayashi Corporation, "Restoring the World's Largest Wooden Building Built by Hideyoshi in Kyoto, Hōkō-ji Daibutsuden," 2016.</ref>.]] [[File:Higashiyamameishozue(hokoji).jpg|thumb|250px|A depiction of the second Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji, built by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|en|Toyotomi Hideyori|qid=Q313641|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}} (from the "Higashiyama Meisho Zue," Kyoto Prefecture Historical Archives Digital Collection)]] [[File:Hokoji-heimenzu.png|thumb|250px|Blueprint of the second Great Buddha Hall of Hōkō-ji. The octagonal structure at the center is the Kongō-enclosure (railing), and the circular part is the double lotus pedestal of the Great Buddha. The idea that the pedestal was octagonal is incorrect<ref name="#1">Kyoto City Archaeological Research Institute, "Hoshoji-den Site, Rokuhara Administrative Office Site, and Hōkō-ji Site," 2010 (2009-8), p. 67.</ref><ref name="Choso2020">Choso Shigeichi, "Proposal for Reconstruction of the Great Buddha Hall: An Approach Based on Excavation Findings," *Nihonshi Kenkyu,* Issue 698, October 2020, p. 59.</ref>. In earlier reports, the base was described as octagonal, but subsequent analysis revised this to a nearly circular polygon<ref name="#1" />).]] [[File:Timeline of daibutsu in Japan.svg|thumb|250px|Timeline of the Great Buddha of Kyoto's existence]]
The '''Great Buddha of Kyoto''' (京の大仏, Kyō no Daibutsu) was a {{ill|Japanese|en|Japanese|qid=Q346080|lt=Japanese|1=Japanese}} {{ill|Daibutsu|en|Daibutsu|qid=Q1051606|lt=Great Buddha|1=Daibutsu}} statue that once stood at the {{ill|Hōkō-ji (Kyoto)|en|Hōkō-ji (Kyoto)|qid=Q1045971|lt=Hōkō-ji|1=Hōkō-ji (Kyoto)}} temple in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, now part of [[:en:Higashiyama Ward|Higashiyama Ward]], Kyoto City.
From the first to the fourth Great Buddha, the statue underwent several iterations. The fourth Great Buddha was destroyed by fire, and none of the statues survive today. The third Great Buddha was once considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Buddhas of Japan|ja|日本三大仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Buddhas of Japan|1=Three Great Buddhas of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11507108}}<ref name="Diagram">{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Kumiko Yakushiji|title=Photographic and Illustrated Guide to Japanese Buddhist Statues|publisher=Seitosha|year=2016|page=170}}</ref>.
The first Great Buddha was commissioned by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} in the 5th year of {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|qid=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku|1=Bunroku}} (1595){{Sfn|Kawachi|2008|p=19}} but was later dismantled{{Sfn|Kawachi|2008|p=dd5}}. The second Great Buddha was completed in {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 17 (1612){{Sfn|Kawachi|2023|p=145}}, but it was damaged in the {{ill|1662 Kanbun earthquake|en|1662 Kanbun earthquake|qid=Q11457492|lt=Kanbun Earthquake|1=1662 Kanbun earthquake}} of 1662{{Sfn|Murayama|2003|p=148}}. The third Great Buddha, rebuilt in {{ill|Kanbun|en|Kanbun|qid=Q7901319|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun}} 7 (1667), was destroyed by lightning in {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|qid=Q1195131|lt=Kansei|1=Kansei}} 10 (1798){{Sfn|Murayama|2003|p=157}}. The fourth Great Buddha, constructed in {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|qid=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 14 (1843){{Sfn|Murayama|2003|p=162}}, was consumed by fire in 1973 (Showa 48){{Sfn|Murayama|2003|p=163}}.
[[ja:京の大仏]]
{{translated page|ja|京の大仏|version=102537748|comment=Imported full ja history}} <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Hokoji Daibutsu}} {{wikidata link|Q11374178}} [[Category:Articles to be expanded]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Colossal Buddha statues in Japan]] [[Category:History of Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kyoto in the Azuchi–Momoyama Period]] [[Category:Kyoto in the Edo period]] [[Category:Statues of Vairocana Buddha]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamashiro Province]]
